title: Which app can be used to annotate screenshots and convert them into monday.com tasks?

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

I’m curious, which apps do you usually use to take screenshots, annotate them, and then convert those screenshots into tasks in monday.com?

I’ve been exploring different tools. Of course, we all know the classic options like Paint or Figma (super powerful, but feels like overkill for a quick bug report). But I’d love to hear what works best for you in your daily workflow.

Recently, I came across this blog article that compares different approaches. It also mentions a native monday.com marketplace app called Capture that lets you:

  • Take a screenshot instantly

  • Annotate screenshots with arrows, text & highlights

  • Blur sensitive data

  • Share via link

  • Convert screenshots directly into monday.com tasks

And actually, that’s the reason our team decided to build Capture as a monday.com add-on. At first, we created it just for ourselves - because we were tired of the messy workflow of uploading static screenshots and explaining everything manually. Over time, we realized others might need the same solution, so we released it publicly. That’s why it’s a truly working product, we use it every day ourselves.

By the way, If it’s not installed yet, try it today with a free 14-day trial:
check the step-by-step guide: How to install Capture for monday.com

To use Capture, you must have:

  1. A monday.com account with the Capture for monday.com app installed.

  2. Capture in Chrome for monday.com from the Chrome Web Store.

So, what screenshot tools or monday.com apps do you use to report bugs, give feedback, or create tasks faster?

Looking forward to your recommendations!
